issue_title: Allow easy customization of waiter delay/iteration count
issue_body:
I'm not sure about others, but sometimes the default waiter delay/count is insufficient for me. For example, the recently added CloudFormation waiters are great (I like that the state modeling and polling machinery is done for me) but the default delay is 30 seconds between polls, which significantly slows things down for my fairly small stacks. In other cases, I've had waiters give up on a particularly slow operation because the max poll count was too low, even though I knew ahead of time it would be slow and would just have increased the max poll time if I could've.

1. Is it even possible to adjust those parameters today?
2. If it is possible, can it be made easier and part of the official (documented) interface for waiters?

issue_title: EC2 ImageAvailable Waiter contains an unreachable failure case
issue_body:
The [ec2/2014-10-01.waiters.json](https://github.com/boto/botocore/blob/75be2f130e40d02f004f22b55f236d4ef5fd10f4/botocore/data/aws/ec2/2014-10-01.waiters.json#L140) file currently contains the expected match of `"failed"` for the failure state.

Is this actually correct? It appears that the  match should be `"deregistered"` to match the failure case against the actual `ImageState` [enumeration values](https://github.com/boto/botocore/blob/75be2f130e40d02f004f22b55f236d4ef5fd10f4/botocore/data/aws/ec2/2014-10-01.normal.json#L6454).
TeXKeys are automatically generated, shouldn't be deleted, but can be declared obsolete by a cataloger.

issue_title: schema: /acquisition_source/datetime does not have a type
Invalid/unknown component name being substituted during parsing
issue_body:
Hi,

I encountered a strange behavior in a case where there are invalid component names in an iCalendar string.

```python
>>> # the following probably shouldn't pass
>>> vcal = icalendar.Calendar.from_ical('BEGIN:THISWRONG\nSTRANGEPROP:12345\nEND:THISWRONG')
>>> # but it did
>>> vcal.to_ical()
'BEGIN:VCALENDAR\r\nSTRANGEPROP:12345\r\nEND:VCALENDAR\r\n'
```

As you can see, the parser accepted the component "THISWRONG" without any warning/error. On top of that, it represents it as a VCALENDAR component instead. This does not seem to be a correct behavior at all. It is caused by:

https://github.com/collective/icalendar/blob/master/src/icalendar/cal.py#L328

Also notice the property "STRANGEPROP" from the example. The case here is similar as "STRANGEPROP" is definitely not defined in iCalendar RFC. At least here it stays the same "STRANGEPROP" property, though.

I understand that this behavior might be there for the sake of extensibility, but at least in the first case it is not handled correctly at all. Perhaps a pluggable interface would help here but until then at least the first case should be fixed.

Opinions/comments are welcome :)

https://github.com/DataDog/dd-trace-py/blob/261136e112b23862a78308a2423e15364ae4aaa6/ddtrace/contrib/pyramid/trace.py#L31

Here we're removing request from kwargs but pyramid has a request kwarg.

foo

foobaz

', format='html') ``` Or maybe I am missing something?

Hi,

It's not documented but it seems the default encoding for the signature name is ASCII: https://github.com/libgit2/pygit2/blob/master/src/signature.c#L41

Is there a particular reason why it's not UTF-8, like about everything else in git/libgit2?

When I pass a unicode/text object, I wouldn't expect to provide the encoding as well (there is an accent in my name).

Especially when it seems to be the default for internally built signatures: https://github.com/libgit2/pygit2/blob/master/src/signature.c#L114

issue_body:
Python's `zipfile.ZipFile` contains a few issues that could be exploited if this code is used on a server that accepts egg uploads.

* It reads all zip index entries into memory. For specially crafted zips, this can consume far more memory than the size of the whole zip file.
* If a zip is crafted to contain many small files, this can be used as denial of service as scanning the whole zip file can take a long time.

For extracting just egg metadata, scan of zip archive looking for only relevant entries. Perhaps also limit the number of files scanned.

issue_title: Continue to allow access to private methods?
issue_body:
Per discussion with @pekkaklarck on the Slack group...

## Essential Issue

Having created some Python helper functions, leveraging the **Selenium2Library**, I have been able to leverage some of the private methods to accomplish some useful things for my testing framework. An example below shows a function `get_column_number` which used to be able to return the column number of whatever (valid) column _name_ was passed in:

```python
from robot.libraries.BuiltIn import BuiltIn

def _get_sel2lib():
    """== Gets Current Selenium 2 Instance from Robot Framework ==
    
    - Uses the Robot Framework API to get an object of the current Selenium 2 instance.
    - This is an internal helper function only. 
    
    == Calling ==
    
    | *Args* | [none] | |
    | *Returns* | ``object`` | An object instance of the current Selenium 2 library.
    | *Raises* | [none] | |
    """
    return BuiltIn().get_library_instance('Selenium2Library')

def get_column_number(table_locator, col_text, loglevel='INFO'):
    """== Returns Number of Specified Column ==
    
    Returns the number of the first column found which contains the ``col_text`` string.

    - Does not require an exact match; if there is a column named ``Foobar`` and you input ``Foo`` then it will match.
    
    === Calling ===
    
    | *Args* | ``table_locator`` (str) | The table locator containing the column whose number you desire. |
    |        | ``col_text`` (str) | The string of the column you want to locate. |
    | *Returns* | ``int`` | Number of column containing string ``col_name``. |
    | *Raises* | AssertionError | If ``col_name`` cannot be found in the table specified. |

    === Example in Robot ===

    | ${column_number} =    Get Column Number    xpath=//*[@id="deviceListHeader"]    Unsafe
    """
    sel2lib = _get_sel2lib()
    locators = sel2lib._table_element_finder._parse_table_locator(table_locator, 'header')
    found_it = False
    for locator in locators:
        elements = sel2lib._element_finder.find(sel2lib._current_browser(), locator)
        counter = 0
        for element in elements:
            counter += 1
            if col_text in element.text:
                output = counter
                found_it = True
                break
    if found_it is False:
        sel2lib.log_source(loglevel)
        raise AssertionError("No column containing '{}' found in the table identified via {}!".format(col_text, table_locator))
    return output
```

With the _new_ **SeleniumLibrary**, I get an error: `AttributeError: 'SeleniumLibrary' object has no attribute '_table_element_finder'` as that structure doesn't exist as-is. I do notice, however, that [in tableelementfinder.py there is a method named `find_by_header`](https://github.com/robotframework/SeleniumLibrary/blob/master/src/SeleniumLibrary/locators/tableelementfinder.py#L70) which I could leverage _if_ I could access it.

## General Request

Provide a means to access some of these methods to get more granular access to some of the inner workings of the Selenium library.

I would like to propose my help in implementing this feature: if one package needs to be frozen to a preversion (and only one), allow the dependency finding to pick this one even without the `--pre` parameter.

The `--pre` parameter which the preversion search for **all** packages, and sometimes we only want one package

issue_title: Pipenv does not lock dependencies of installed wheels
issue_body:
When installing a dependency through a wheel (using either `https://` or `file://`), the initial installation will work fine and install all the library's dependencies into your virtual env. Running `pipenv graph` will show the dependency tree correctly. The resulting `Pipenv.lock` file though, will not have the child dependencies of the wheel (even after running `pipenv lock` again).

I created a repo that shows the error happening in one script: https://github.com/TotempaaltJ/minimal-pipenv-wheel-fail. issue_title: Setuptools' sdist doesn't use data_files while distutils does
issue_body:
Originally reported by: **ionelmc (Bitbucket: [ionelmc](http://bitbucket.org/ionelmc), GitHub: [ionelmc](http://github.com/ionelmc))**

----------------------------------------

Distutils has this: https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/master/Lib/distutils/command/sdist.py#L270-L282

However that code is not copies in the sdist provided by setuptools. 

This can be problematic if you don't inlcude a file in MANIFEST.in but you have it in data_files - python's docs will most certainly mislead you as they say that sdist uses data_files: https://docs.python.org/3/distutils/sourcedist.html?highlight=data_files#specifying-the-files-to-distribute (last bullet)

----------------------------------------
- Bitbucket: https://bitbucket.org/pypa/setuptools/issue/521

issue_title: Dealing with deprecation of ez_setup.py for non-install commands
issue_body: The documentation of setuptools points out that ``ez_setup.py`` is deprecated in favor of ``pyproject.toml`` and pip. If I am currently in a directory with a Python package that uses ez_setup.py, I can run any ``python setup.py <command>`` and have setuptools be temporarily 'installed' if needed. Once there is a stable release of pip that supports ``pyproject.toml``, it will be possible to do ``pip install .`` instead of ``python setup.py install``, but this doesn't solve the issue of how to deal with other commands. For example, if I run ``python setup.py test`` there is no such thing as using pip, so how does one ensure that setuptools is installed in this scenario?

Ryan has a sqlite3 db that maps rsid <-> cpra.  He also has a table mapping old rsids to current ones.  Use those to fix up association files that are missing some cpras.

- [ ] bgzip: pysam.tabix_compress(infile, outfile) - it calls straight down to C on the filenames.  But I want to stream data into it.
    - Maybe call it in a subprocess `python -c '...'` and use `/dev/fd/0`

issue_title: POST with TLS causes double-close on body producer
issue_body:
This appears to be caused by the body producer's `stopProducing` method being called both by the TCP protocol and the TLS protocol:

```

Location: /Users/dmurvihill/.venv/txbrake/lib/python2.7/site-packages
Requires: incremental, requests, pyOpenSSL, six, service-identity, Twisted
```
```
$ pip show twisted
Name: Twisted
Version: 16.6.0
Summary: An asynchronous networking framework written in Python
Home-page: http://twistedmatrix.com/
Author: Glyph Lefkowitz
Author-email: glyph@twistedmatrix.com
License: MIT
Location: /Users/dmurvihill/.venv/txbrake/lib/python2.7/site-packages
Requires: constantly, zope.interface, incremental
```
```
$ cat test_treq_tls.py 
import treq
from twisted.internet import reactor                                                                       
treq.post('https://requestb.in/19qt4ei1', data=b'testdata').addBoth(lambda _: reactor.stop())
reactor.run()
```
```
$ python test_treq_tls.py 
Unexpected exception from twisted.web.client.FileBodyProducer.stopProducing
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Users/dmurvihill/.venv/txbrake/lib/python2.7/site-packages/twisted/internet/endpoints.py", line 130, in connectionLost

  File "/Users/dmurvihill/.venv/txbrake/lib/python2.7/site-packages/twisted/web/_newclient.py", line 929, in dispatcher

  File "/Users/dmurvihill/.venv/txbrake/lib/python2.7/site-packages/twisted/web/_newclient.py", line 1599, in _connectionLost_TRANSMITTING
    self._currentRequest.stopWriting()
  File "/Users/dmurvihill/.venv/txbrake/lib/python2.7/site-packages/twisted/web/_newclient.py", line 830, in stopWriting
    _callAppFunction(self.bodyProducer.stopProducing)
--- <exception caught here> ---
  File "/Users/dmurvihill/.venv/txbrake/lib/python2.7/site-packages/twisted/web/_newclient.py", line 194, in _callAppFunction

  File "/Users/dmurvihill/.venv/txbrake/lib/python2.7/site-packages/twisted/web/client.py", line 1041, in stopProducing

    raise self._completionState

```

Occurs on other versions of Twisted and Treq as well.

Both calls appear to originate in this method of `tcp.Connection`:
```python
    def connectionLost(self, reason):
        """See abstract.FileDescriptor.connectionLost().
        """
        # Make sure we're not called twice, which can happen e.g. if
        # abortConnection() is called from protocol's dataReceived and then
        # code immediately after throws an exception that reaches the
        # reactor. We can't rely on "disconnected" attribute for this check
        # since twisted.internet._oldtls does evil things to it:
        if not hasattr(self, "socket"):
            return
        abstract.FileDescriptor.connectionLost(self, reason)  # (DM) first close occurs here
        self._closeSocket(not reason.check(error.ConnectionAborted))
        protocol = self.protocol
        del self.protocol
        del self.socket
        del self.fileno
        protocol.connectionLost(reason)  # (DM) second close occurs here
```

issue_title: change "-i" parameter to be comma separated values rather than space
issue_body: change "-i" parameter to be comma separated values rather than space separated to avoid confusion in params (see email to Anubhav from Anton Berg). You will also need to search for "space-separated" in the docs and update those.

issue_title: Support N-dimensional arrays in blob detection
issue_body:
## Description

I have some N-dimensional blobs that need detecting, and skimage has the nicest implementation of Laplacian of Gaussian blob detection I've seen in a major python library - but only supports 2D. From a cursory glance, it doesn't look like any of the core logic would break for the N-D case. 

So rather than writing my own version which would be nearly identical to `skimage.feature.blob_log` aside from a missing `assert_nD(input, 2)`, perhaps `skimage` could add support for the N-D case?

Could someone more familiar with `skimage` comment as to whether there would be any major obstacles encountered in modifying the existing logic to handle this? 

I realize that it's scikit-*image*, but who says that "image" strictly refers to 2D and doesn't also describe, say, volumetric electron microsopy data?

## Description
Revert https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/pull/2774 once https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/pull/2766 is merged.

Maybe I didn't understand properly the code and method but I suspect small mistake in the code.

In the module skimage.feature exist function  - hessian_matrix_eigvals (line 257).

This function should calculate eigenvalues of the hessian matrix.
It is done by calling _image_orthogonal_matrix22_eigvals function in line 291.
 
But the arguments which are given to the function which calculates eigenvalues are : Hyy, Hxy, Hyy. 
It is my question. It is ok?

In my opinion there is a mistake, because these agruments shuld be: Hxx, Hxy, Hyy.

Thank you in advance for your time and response.

Many modules use `import` statements haphazardly and do not seem to follow a standard protocol. Some pull dependencies using relative pathways:

`from ..measure._label import label`

Whereas others use absolute ones:

`from skimage.measure._label import label`

1. Which should be the standard?
2. Additionally, sometimes files draw on entire modules when all they require are a couple of functions, should this be changed?
3. What is the protocol when only a single function is using a module? I am not a fan of importing within function definitions and would rather have the `import` at the beginning of the file. Example (from `skimage.morphology.selem`):

```
def octagon(m, n, dtype=np.uint8):

    ...

    return selem
```

issue_title: Move repeated read / write logic to base Serializable class

issue_title: Have nupic.core build output a shared library artifact instead of the static lib
issue_body:
Currently, nupic_core_solo is a static library and gets repackaged with the external dependencies into a combined static library that the binaries and Python extensions link to.

This change is to:

- Remove the combined nupic_core static library
- Change nupic_core_solo to a shared library and include the external dependencies for linking
- Change the extensions to dynamically link to the nupic_core shared library
- It looks like the logical place to put the Python-specific files (called {{src_py_support_files}} in {{src/CMakeLists.txt}}) is in nupic_core_solo but it may be better to leave them out and include them when compiled the Python extensions since they aren't really part of the C++ API that we want to expose in the shared library. But whoever is looking into this can make a judgement call.

issue_title: Lots of GCC "maybe-uninitialized" false positives on Windows
issue_body:
I keep having to disable the "maybe-uninitialized" GCC check. It keeps having false positives.

- https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/commit/e193bbe030f738eeee9a534970d59a1e13b3e9c8
- https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/commit/92a04f5e3e5018e75d232010456347c77ff74065

I'm running into it a third time in https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/pull/1018

We apparently are only using this check in our Windows build. I think we should disable this check in the build via `-Wno-maybe-uninitialized`.

Thoughts, @vitaly-krugl and @scottpurdy ?

In our implementation, every information bound on the logger is shared by thread.

This allows us to bind in some place and dump out in some other place:
* https://github.com/mozilla-services/cliquet/blob/master/cliquet/resource.py#L67
* https://github.com/mozilla-services/cliquet/blob/master/cliquet/logs.py#L69

Should this be changed for a logger local the request instead ?
``request.registry.logger = logger.bind(user_id=...)``

Also, the subrequests logging could be implemented differently. With the current implementation, subrequests logging overwrites the bound info of the main request logger (the one coming from POST /batch).

See https://github.com/mozilla-services/cliquet/blob/master/cliquet/views/batch.py#L118-L123

This is related to this posting on SO:  http://stackoverflow.com/questions/30736627/cornice-schema-validation-with-colanderalchemy/30761861#30761861

Essentially validation will only work if the colander schema passed in is a direct subclass of `MappingSchema`.  Using something like `SchemaNode(typ=Mapping)` should work just fine, but instead it throws `cornice.schemas.SchemaError: schema is not a MappingSchema`.

issue_title: ipywidgets stop working after enabling qgrid
issue_body:
### Environment

* Operating System: OSX
* Python Version: 3.6
* How did you install Qgrid: pip
* Python packages: notebook 5.2.2, jupyterlab 0.31.0, qgrid 1.0.0, ipywidgets 7.1.0
* Jupyter lab packages (if applicable): @jupyter-widgets/jupyterlab-manager v0.33.0, qgrid v1.0.0

### Description of Issue

ipywidgets fail to render in JupyterLab after installing/enabling the qgrid Lab extension.

### Reproduction Steps

1. Open a notebook in jupyterlab.
2. `import qgrid; qgrid.show_grid(a_df)`
3. Grid appears (but with some errors in the JS console)
4. `import ipywidgets; ipywidgets.Button(description="test")`
5. "Error displaying widget` appears in the notebook
6. Stop lab, run `jupyter labextension disable qgrid`, restart lab
7. Try the ipywidgets test again
8. Button appears as expected

### What steps have you taken to resolve this already?

> Please Note: Jupyterlab support has been tested with jupyterlab 0.30.5 and jupyterlab-manager 0.31.3, so if you're having trouble, try installing those versions. Feel free to file an issue if you find that qgrid isn't working with a newer version of either dependency.

I can fallback on older versions of lab and widgets-manager. I'm opening this ticket to raise awareness about a likely problem with the latest versions.

Hello RDFLib

In trying to re-implement a system using rdflib I am facing a key capability gap: I require the ability to run queries that have SERVICE calls within the Query: federated queries, as defined by W3 in:

https://www.w3.org/TR/sparql11-federated-query/

Using rdflib (v4.2.2) I receive 
`Exception: ServiceGraphPattern not implemented`
errors, from
https://github.com/RDFLib/rdflib/blob/58d6369a12791ae7c1e259c86aeea384390d36ee/rdflib/plugins/sparql/evaluate.py#L262

#484 suggests that this has been the case for some time.

I am interested in looking to implement this capability and I would like to start a discussion on how amenable RDFLib developers would be to an enhancement in this space?

I would also very much appreciate some advice on how to approach an implementation: what are the key factors to be aware of? what are the implementation and testing preferences within the developer community?  

It strikes me that if it were very simple to do, it may have been done by now, so I would like to explore some of the potential complexities in principal before putting too much time into an implementation that will never fit with the RDFLib approach or simply never work.

I will add further comments on key details as I find them and explore implementation ideas with you as this ticket develops.

Your thoughts on whether this capability is of interest to RDFLib and how to implement it effectively are hugely welcome at this stage.

many thanks
mark

issue_title: Add multiple Vault-type credentials on creation of new Job Template
issue_body:
When using `tower-cli job_template create` there is no known way to add multiple `vault-credential` to the template.  To date, I have tried multiple `--vault-credential "<cred_name>"` arguments and the template simply uses the last specified argument.  I've also tried a comma-delimited list in a single argument (e.g. `--vault-credential "<cred1_name>,<cred2_name>"`, but it errors as it cannot find a single credential matching  the combination of the names.

Given that Ansible 2.4+ supports multiple vaults, I need a method to add the vaults to the job template at create time.  The AWX GUI does allow you to specify multiple vault credentials by clicking the Search icon next to the Credential field, filtering by Vault type, and checking multiple credential items in the list.
Currently python 3.7 is unsupported. Is it to early to introduce support right now?

### Description

After seeing #195 and taking the whole node left-pad debacle we should consider vendorizing at least some our dependencies like [pip](https://github.com/pypa/pip/tree/develop/pip/_vendor) and [requests](https://github.com/kennethreitz/requests/tree/master/requests/packages), among others, do.

### Pros

- We control the versions that we use, and are not dependent on what's is or is not in pypi;
- We are not vulnerable to packages getting removed or replaced on pypi.

### Cons

- We need to vendorize packages;
- We need to keep those packages updated.

issue_title: Raise original exception while importing modules
issue_body:
### Description

Currently, when importing Python modules dynamically while binding routes to Flask, if something is wrong with the loaded code there's only a generic `connexion` exception raised, so the original error is hidden and therefore there's not easy way to see what was the real error.

### Expected behaviour

Catch the original Python exception and raise it properly exposing it to higher layers instead of raising a generic error who tells you nothing.

### Actual behaviour

This is the error stack trace, which is not exposing the real problem:
```
ERROR:connexion.api:Failed to add operation for DELETE /api/reports/{report}
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Users/taparicio/.pyenv/versions/3.5.1/lib/python3.5/site-packages/connexion/api.py", line 196, in add_paths
    self.add_operation(method, path, endpoint, path_parameters)
  File "/Users/taparicio/.pyenv/versions/3.5.1/lib/python3.5/site-packages/connexion/api.py", line 169, in add_operation
    resolver=self.resolver)
  File "/Users/taparicio/.pyenv/versions/3.5.1/lib/python3.5/site-packages/connexion/operation.py", line 172, in __init__
    resolution = resolver.resolve(self)
  File "/Users/taparicio/.pyenv/versions/3.5.1/lib/python3.5/site-packages/connexion/resolver.py", line 51, in resolve
    return Resolution(self.resolve_function_from_operation_id(operation_id), operation_id)
  File "/Users/taparicio/.pyenv/versions/3.5.1/lib/python3.5/site-packages/connexion/resolver.py", line 72, in resolve_function_from_operation_id
    return self.function_resolver(operation_id)
  File "/Users/taparicio/.pyenv/versions/3.5.1/lib/python3.5/site-packages/connexion/utils.py", line 109, in get_function_from_name
    function = deep_getattr(module, attr_path)
  File "/Users/taparicio/.pyenv/versions/3.5.1/lib/python3.5/site-packages/connexion/utils.py", line 91, in deep_getattr
    return functools.reduce(getattr, attr.split('.'), obj)
AttributeError: module 'api' has no attribute 'reports'
```

### Steps to reproduce

Just try breaking your dynamically loaded code.

### Additional info:

Output of the commands:

- Python `3.5.1`.
- Version: 1.0.98
Swagger ui should be optional.

issue_title: Providing username and/or password in URI raises InvalidURI when using websocket
select query fails, if query contains '$' symbol
issue_body:
`list(db.select("SELECT value FROM $table WHERE key ='$utm_source'", model_class=CHEventProperty))`
raises Exception
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/code.py", line 91, in runcode
    exec(code, self.locals)
  File "<input>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/home/sergei/venv36/lib/python3.6/site-packages/infi/clickhouse_orm/database.py", line 157, in select
    query = self._substitute(query, model_class)
  File "/home/sergei/venv36/lib/python3.6/site-packages/infi/clickhouse_orm/database.py", line 273, in _substitute
    query = Template(query).substitute(mapping)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/string.py", line 126, in substitute
    return self.pattern.sub(convert, self.template)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/string.py", line 119, in convert
    return str(mapping[named])
KeyError: 'utm_source'
```

The problem is `database._substitute()` method which uses `Template(query).substitute(mapping)`. Template expects all keys mentioned in query being in mapping. Suppose, [safe_substitute()](https://docs.python.org/2/library/string.html#string.Template.safe_substitute) method should be used here.

``` 
Nous avons reproduit ce comportement sur le simulateur embauche.beta.gouv.fr.

Ci dessous un premier appel (celui avec les paramètres par défaut) sur une simulation d'embauche en novembre 2017 :
```

issue_title: Considering the usage of strip_url to name a node as a none perfect solution
issue_body:
Right now most of the extensions that implement patches or machinery to allow instrumentalize HTTP clients such as Aiohttp, Requests or HTTPLib use by default the `strip_url` as fallback when there is no name given [1] or even as a primary way to name a subsegment [2][3].

Relying on the output of this function when there is no an alternative name, the number of nodes that are created in the AWS Xray console is as many different URLrs has been generated, so the following URLs will end up creating different nodes each one. While common sense says that all of them refer to the same service:

```python
>>> strip_url("http://localhost/resource/1?debug=true")
'http://localhost/resource/1'
>>> strip_url("http://localhost/resource/2")
'http://localhost/resource/2'
>>> strip_url("http://localhost/resource/1,2,3")
'http://localhost/resource/1,2,3'
>>> strip_url("http://localhost/resource/1,2,3")
'http://localhost/resource/1,2,3'
````

So, IMO we must address this situation or at least give to the user a way of inferring the name the segment based on the URL hosts or any strategy that allows the user to group those queries that belong to the same node. Taking as example the previous set of URLs all of them will be named as `http://localhost`.

I guess there is no a silver bullet for that, but before start digging into a proper solution I would like to gather your feedback about the issue by itself, and ofc if you have any proposal I will like to hear them


[1] https://github.com/aws/aws-xray-sdk-python/blob/master/aws_xray_sdk/ext/aiohttp/client.py#L25
[2] https://github.com/aws/aws-xray-sdk-python/blob/master/aws_xray_sdk/ext/requests/patch.py#L29
[3] https://github.com/aws/aws-xray-sdk-python/blob/master/aws_xray_sdk/ext/httplib/patch.py#L54

`
up = upyun.UpYun('****', secret='*****', endpoint=upyun.ED_AUTO)

`

I think we should consider formalizing a file format for manticore workspaces.

Currently data is spread across many folders as each run creates a workspace folder and respective workspace files. This proposal would be to consolidate runs and individual files into a single workspace file/db that is exposed via API alongside a companion CLI for reviewing logs / exporting data on the command line. I saw there is or was support for redis? but this adds a requirement of running a redis instance which is undesirable. 

The idea is to consolidate output from multiple sessions into a single file, add APIs for interacting with workspace data while not limiting or hindering the ability to filter or pipe that data to others tools from the CLI. 

This should be discussed more in depth but my thoughts are something along the lines of a CLI (backed by an API) util that acts as a swiss army knife for extracting session information and managing session data across all possible sessions. I think this could potentially benefit integration efforts of other projects that will be consuming manticore output.

It would be nice if DyNet gave you some way to easily profile which nodes were using lots of memory in the computation graph.

issue_title: Training with autobatching seems to be slower than before
issue_body:
I suspect that training with autobatching got a bit slower after some recent commit. 
I compared some examples (train_rnn-autobatch in examples, bilstm-tagger-withchar-bulk, treenn-bulk and rnnlm-batch in neulab/dynet-benchmark) between the latest (a69c515) and an old (a3782a1) commit, and found that old ones were 1.4 ~ 1.8 times faster in all of them. 
I chose a3782a1 for comparison because I remembered it was faster, and I don't think it is _the_ last commit without this speed issue.

The Python attention example (https://github.com/clab/dynet/blob/master/pyexamples/attention.py) is probably broken, as it doesn't look like it is re-inputting the generated output to the decoder LSTM. This is an important part of attentional models, and probably should be fixed.

issue_title: LSTM builder is not the standard LSTM
issue_body:
After looking at the code in lstm.h, it seems that the LSTMBuilder cell has the following update rule : 
![lstm_dynet](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/10391785/20107694/6f0cde70-a5a7-11e6-8e2e-3e8fdad042ea.png)

Which is not the common update rule :

![common_lstm](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/10391785/20107664/567ca0c0-a5a7-11e6-9468-c07caad2020a.png)

(i.e. in the dynet implementation the forget and input gate are coupled and there are peaky connections with the cell state c)

What are the reasons behinf this choice.

Anyway I think this should be documented.

issue_title: The netcdftime package is no longer included with netcdf4 1.4.0
issue_body:
Check this:
https://github.com/Unidata/netcdf4-python

The netcdftime package is no longer included with netcdf4 1.4.0, which cause a ModuleNotFoundError when trying to import pyart after updating netcdf4. 

```python
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<string>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/short/miniconda3/envs/radar/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pyart/__init__.py", line 59, in <module>

```

```
topic = client.topic(PUBSUB_TOPIC_NAME)
sub = topic.subscription(PUBSUB_SUB_NAME)
if not topic.exists():

if not sub.exists():

while True:
```

issue_title: processing_rate is a multiplier and not absolute number
issue_body:
When I pass `"processing_rate":1` as part of `mapper_params` and examine the logs of `/mapreduce/worker_callback` I see that each worker callback processes **8** entities each time. If I set  `"processing_rate":2` each callback will process **16** entities. On another project I've worked on, the numbers were **15** and **30** (for `processing_rate` of 1 and 2). So I conclude that `processing_rate` param is a multiplier.
* Where can I set the actual value?
* What makes it change from project to project?

issue_title: dill fails (_locate_function) to pickle a function created with eval()
issue_body:
a fix is simple in the failing function `_locate_function` add a check if the `obj.__module__ is None`:
```
def _locate_function(obj, session=False):
    if not obj.__module__ or obj.__module__ == '__main__': # and session:
        return False
    found = _import_module(obj.__module__ + '.' + obj.__name__, safe=True)
    return found is obj
```

issue_title: Dropdown Menus stopped working after adding Fullcalendar
issue_body:
I have a simple Rails application that I am trying to add Fullcalendar to.  After modifying my application.js file with "//= require moment" and "//= require fullcalendar" my dropdown menus stop working.  When you clicked on them nothing at all happened except for a "#" sign was being added to the end of the URL in the browser's address bar.  After a few days of looking for a solution I found information about the order of things in the application.css and the applicatoin.js files.  It turns out that the above two mentioned requires need to be after "//= require jquery" and "//= require jquery_ujs" lines in the application.js file.  I have no idea why, but I sure other people well run into the same problem.

I hope that this information helps.

issue_title: Allow duck-typing of Requirement in resolvable.py?
issue_body:
https://github.com/pantsbuild/pex/commit/8b7bb05c5ecef5db61ef4f49189d4ecae07a3a70 adds explicit type-checking in function resolvables_from_iterable() in resolver.py: a requirement must be a Resolvable, Requirement, Package or string. 

However Pants uses this method and passes in a PythonRequirement, which is a Pants-specific class that wraps Requirement and duck-types to it. 

So we should consider allowing this method to accept duck types, or change Pants appropriately.

I would like to suggest the addition of an `additional_build_roots` option to pantsbuild. If this is already an option, please forgive my ignorance. 

## Motivation
As pantsbuild becomes more popular, we may expect that people who are writing applications in pantsbuild might want to leverage already existing pantsbuild applications. In the case that there is no other way to retrieve these applications, there should be an easy way to reference already existing pantsbuild setups within an existing project.

## The Problem
One might be able to see the dilemma. If I have my project, projectA, and an external library (which uses pantsbuild itself), libraryExt, I would attempt to integrate the two together putting their folders at the same level (Such that `projectA/` and `libraryExt/` are both in the build root).

Then, in order to depend on something that libraryExt provides, I insert something like `dependencies = [ "//libraryExt/src/python:feature" ]` into a `BUILD` file in projectA.

This is all well and good; however, when pants resolves `//libraryExt/src/python:feature`, it will then attempt to resolve dependencies of `//libraryExt/src/python:feature`, which are defined relative to libraryExt's build root. Pants will then exit and inform the user that it cannot find those targets.

## Current Solution
I have solved this problem by writing a generic script that rewrites all subproject's BUILD targets as relative to the parent project's build path.

## Proposed Solution
The addition of a `additional_build_roots` option, which takes a list of paths of other build roots. Upon not being able to resolve a target, pants will then attempt to resolve the target starting from roots listed in `additional_build_roots`.

## Possible Complications/Solutions

### Incorrect Target Resolution
#### Problem
This option could create some interesting behavior between same-named targets in different build roots. Consider the following scenario:

`projectA/src/python/BUILD` has `dependencies = [ "//src/python:nonexistent" ]`, which doesn't exist and should not exist.
`projectB/src/python/BUILD` contains the definition for the target "nonexistent" and `projectB/` is also enumerated as an `additional_build_root` in the options.

Thus, pants will successfully resolve the `nonexistent` target by finding it in projectB, even though this was not the intended behavior.

#### Solution
This problem presents itself because a target was resolved using a build root that it did not fall under. The purpose of this `additional_build_roots` is not to allow for cross-build-root behavior, but instead to resolve targets within the same build root.

Thus, pants should only attempt to resolve an unresolved target using an `additional_build_root` if the `BUILD` file that the target is called from also falls under the `additional_build_root`. 

# Conclusion
This feature would easily allow pants projects to be embedded within another. For single, monolithic projects, this does not have any unintended consequences. However, for projects with embedded subprojects (perhaps using Git Submodules, where one does not want to edit any files away from upstream), this allows clean integration.

issue_title: Support CloudWatch log settings for APIGateway stages
issue_body:
Method settings such as `LoggingLevel` or `DataTraceEnabled` don't appear to be supported in `AWS::Serverless::Api`.

These are the workarounds I've found:
1. Enabling logs manually [via the console](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/apigateway/latest/developerguide/set-up-logging.html) as suggested in [this comment](https://github.com/awslabs/serverless-application-model/issues/53#issuecomment-270262348) by @sanathkr 
2. Enabling logs [via the aws cli](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/apigateway/update-stage.html), e.g:
```
aws apigateway update-stage --rest-api-id xxx --stage-name Prod \
--patch-operations "op=replace,path=/*/*/logging/dataTrace,value=true" \
"op=replace,path=/*/*/logging/loglevel,value=ERROR"
```
3. [AWS::ApiGateway::Stage](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/aws-resource-apigateway-stage.html) supports [MethodSettings](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/aws-properties-apigateway-stage-methodsetting.html), so there may be a workaround where the Stage is defined via `AWS::ApiGateway::Stage` and `AWS::Serverless::Api`'s `StageName` references it?

I've not tested the third option yet, but that would be preferable since the settings are defined in the template which wouldn't require a post deployment step.

It would be great to seem this supported in SAM, in the meantime if anyone has any better suggestions please let me know!

issue_title: mimwrite breaks if input is a generator
issue_body:
mimwrite breaks if `ims` is a generator because of this section:
```python
    #Ensure non empty sequence
    if len(ims) == 0:
        raise ValueError('Numpy sequence must not be empty.')
```
since generators don't have a `__len__` attribute. 

If it's necessary to check for empty sequence, [this workaround](https://stackoverflow.com/a/664239/6038094) could be used for generator types.

issue_labels: [{'id': 492822760, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/learningequality/kolibri/labels/DEV:%20backend', 'name': 'DEV: backend', 'color': 'DFDFDF', 'default': False}, {'id': 492821376, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/learningequality/kolibri/labels/DEV:%20frontend', 'name': 'DEV: frontend', 'color': '1d76db', 'default': False}, {'id': 447172036, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/learningequality/kolibri/labels/TASK:%20bug', 'name': 'TASK: bug', 'color': 'b60205', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
## How to reproduce

1. Start server and navigate to management/content tab. 
2. Import content from internet using content channel id.
3. The progress indicator appears, disappears, and then reappears
4. May need to repeat to see behavior. 

The length of time that the progress indicator is hidden for is longer on slower computers.

## Details

Starting a task (e.g. using `startremoteimport`, `startlocalimport`, and `startlocalexport`) returns a task ID as expected.

However, requesting the list of tasks afterward returns an empty list for a short period of time. This causes the client to have incorrect information about the state of the server.

## User Impact

Behavior feels broken.

More significantly, it's possible that the user - thinking that the transfer did not start - might try to start an import a second time while waiting for the progress indicator to re-appear.

issue_title: "Great work" modal pops up and interrupts reading of an HTML5 app
issue_body:
## Summary

We mark completion and award points for simply spending a short period (10, 15 seconds?) on a PDF or HTML5 app, as we have no better way to track. issue_title: sqlite3-wal got too big file size.
issue_body:
### Observed behavior
After I import the Khan Academy(English) channel and browse its downloadable contents the `sqlite3-wal` changed the file size to almost 1gb. This happened without downloading any contents from Khan Academy(English).

### Errors and logs
![screen shot 2018-07-10 at 2 24 37 pm](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8663934/42493017-cd59d98e-844d-11e8-9430-df5a847ce10f.png)

### Context

 * Kolibri version: 0.11-0a4
 * Operating system: Windows 10
 * Browser: firefox 61.0

## Summary

Ran the script ./kolibri-v0.4.0-beta5.pex language setdefault hi-in, but not successful.

But we tried the other ones available es-es and sw-tz, seams to work fine.

## System information
 - Version: Kolibri 0.4.0beta5
 - Operating system: Ubuntu 14.04 LTS
 - Browser: Chrome

## How to reproduce

1. Run the script for UI switch 

## Screenshots

![language switch](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/13453618/26254962/dd7c3b10-3cd5-11e7-91f9-30e79043ac09.png)

### Observed behaviour
@rtibbles when trying to test #2275 I found this. 

When trying to import a channel it showed some errors showed below
![screen shot 2017-11-22 at 7 54 18 pm](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/13241119/33155516-2805b19e-cfbf-11e7-93bf-e66b752d4d16.png)

![screen shot 2017-11-22 at 7 54 40 pm](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/13241119/33155525-2dbb51a2-cfbf-11e7-8745-90f8e71f06fc.png)

### User-facing consequences

It was not possible to import new channel

### Errors and logs

Relevant errors and tracebacks from:

[log.txt](https://github.com/learningequality/kolibri/files/1497818/log.txt)


### Steps to reproduce

1. Create new branch directly out of current version of develop
2. Access `http://127.0.0.1:8000/management/device/#/content`

### Context

* Kolibri version: 0.6.1 (I believe)
* Operating system: Mac OS High Sierra 10.13.1
* Browser: Chrome Version 62.0.3202.94 (Official Build) (64-bit)

issue_title: Don't let non-superusers reset passwords for superusers
issue_body:
## Summary

We now have a special flag on users to indicate if they are superusers, however, they are also still facility users. This means that currently, a facility admin could reset the password of a superuser in order to gain access to the superuser privileges of this account.

issue_title: Exercise node validation logic doesn't seem to be correct
issue_body:
`or file_formats.PERSEUS` means this will always be true.
```
            files_valid = len(self.files) == 0
            for f in self.files:
                files_valid = files_valid or file_formats.PERSEUS
            assert files_valid , "Assumption Failed: Exercise does not have a .perseus file attached"
```

Also, the logic here (checking all questions are valid) doesn't seem to match the assert text (in fact, if there are no questions, then it specifically *won't* display that message):
```
            # Check if questions are correct
            questions_valid = True
            for q in self.questions:
                questions_valid = questions_valid and q.validate()
            assert questions_valid, "Assumption Failed: Exercise does not have a question"
```

issue_title: Incorrect test for binary mode in _DownloadReportCheckFormat
issue_body:
in adwords.py the method _DownloadReportCheckFormat was recently changed. The new test for is_binary_mode is probably incorrect:

```
  def _DownloadReportCheckFormat(self, file_format, output):
    is_binary_mode = getattr(output, 'mode', 'w') == 'wb'
    if (file_format.startswith('GZIPPED_')
        and not (is_binary_mode or type(output) is io.BytesIO)):
      raise googleads.errors.GoogleAdsValueError('Need to specify a binary'
                                                 ' output for GZIPPED formats.')
```

This will incorrectly raise an exception for some valid binary mode combinations (e.g r+b, w+b).

issue_title: Make `refresh_ha_active()` explicitly set the states of the current and peer firewalls

issue_body:
This should have the ability to move objects that live under a vsys (as well as vsys importables) to the correct vsys object.

Check the vsys params for what's imported.

In the code of `create_nic()`, you have:

```
properties['dhcp'] = str(loadbalancer.dhcp).lower()
```

If the NIC was created with dhcp set to any explession that returns an object, but not True/False, this would lead to sending a string representation of the object to the API. 

Easy fix — convert it to a boolean first:

```
properties['dhcp'] = str(bool(loadbalancer.dhcp)).lower()
```

The same is for `LoadBalancer.dhcp`.

The same is for `NIC.__repr__` and `LoadBalancer.__repr__`.

issue_title: Slow parsing when reading in a large config with multiple neighbors
issue_body:
##### ISSUE TYPE
 - Bug Report

##### OS
RHEL 6.7

##### VERSION
```
ExaBGP : 4.0.2-1c737d99
Python : 3.5.2 (default, Sep 16 2016, 16:05:49)  [GCC 4.4.7 20120313 (Red Hat 4.4.7-3)]

Root   : /root/.local
```
I have also tested the master branch (060cf873dcf876247008c4693fc5095a7213c322) with the same result. I have not tested ExaBGP 3 (incompatible syntax).


##### ENVIRONMENT 
```
The "exabgp --di" output is empty.
```

##### CONFIGURATION
https://gist.github.com/yesbox/12577c779858243594859a73ebcc6348

##### SUMMARY
Parsing a configuration with 6523 routes (mixed IPv4 and IPv6) spread over 16 neighbors (not equally) takes a very long time.

The parsing slows down exponentially for each neighbor config that is being processed. While the configuration is being parsed ExaBGP is running 1 thread at 100% CPU.

Below are some snippets from the debug log to demonstrate the performance characteristics.

```

```

Full debug log excluding the lines between the start and stop (due to being several hundreds of megabyte of lines about timing, which is working without issue once the config is parsed).
https://gist.github.com/yesbox/c3b79a3a7d9f802ad247c9f8383eaa65

Is this a known limitation? Am I doing something in an inefficient way that would be faster with an adjusted config?

##### STEPS TO REPRODUCE
Run exabgp with no arguments other than the config file.

##### EXPECTED RESULTS
Open connections to neighbors in seconds after starting ExaBGP.

##### ACTUAL RESULTS
Time before connections to neighbors are attempted takes hours.

##### IMPORTANCE

issue_title: change the default cu2qu approximation error?
issue_body:
fontmake currently uses the cu2qu default value for the cubic to quadratic conversion, i.e. a factor of 0.0025 of the EM square. Of course, we have an `-e` option to set a custom value, however I wonder if others also share my concern that the current default might be too relaxed and should/could be changed in order to get more accurate results on average, especially for casual users who might not even be aware they can tweak the conversion tolerance.

Related https://github.com/googlei18n/cu2qu/issues/76

issue_title: the Builder.build open mission files with 'r+' flag, i.e. in WRITE mode
issue_body:
which looks like a huge bug to me. 
In salt for example, these .flo files are installed in the python package and are not writable.

add clones attributed to framers to allow references of cloned framers with named clones so that
sub framers can be used with same name but in different super framers. Ie not global name.

The `@subscribe` and `@register` decorators should accept the same options as the corresponding methods in `ApplicationSession`, e.g.:

``` python
@register('foo', RegisterOptions(details_arg='bar', discloseCaller=True))
def baz(bar):
  ..
```

Perhaps even:

``` python
@register('foo', details_arg='bar', discloseCaller=True)
```

This would make code which relies on `RegisterOptions`/`SubscribeOptions` much more readable, since it keeps the annotations inline instead of requiring a fallback to `onJoin` callbacks.

issue_title: Split out the demo from allenai/allennlp
issue_body:
Now that the demo is getting larger and the library is getting more mature, I think it's time to split the demo out from allenai/allennlp.  This will have the added benefit of shrinking our Docker distribution and reducing our build times.  We can depend on `allennlp` via a pip dependency and build a Docker image similar to https://github.com/allenai/allennlp/issues/1324.

We would need to add a build step for the demo repository and update allenai/allennlp-internal to use the new demo Docker images.

**As a user who read the final datapackage,
As a developer, who debug programs, based on DPP,
I want to have fancy printed descriptor, so I could easy read it.**
(Original https://github.com/datahq/datahub-qa/issues/87)

Now any processed descriptor looks not friendly:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11707682/35920293-2f72e382-0c20-11e8-81f4-1543b5f69e3e.png)

## Analysis
It is very easy to fix how json look here:
https://github.com/frictionlessdata/datapackage-pipelines/blob/ccf920c5b574b050776df6dcc433e5a2d853f27b/datapackage_pipelines/lib/dump/dumper_base.py#L200
We could add `indent=2,` and get much more readable output:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11707682/35920438-8b82ac20-0c20-11e8-9fa0-384efda8b3f2.png)

## consequences

- need to fix 3 tests in the DPP
- need to fix a lot of tests in dependent libs & programs
- descriptor file size could increase up to 30% (this will not impact the performance, coz usual descriptor size is few kilobytes)

@akariv what do you think?

@pcarbo and I have decided to give `HDF5` a stab as replacement to current default `RDS` storage format. We start from `R` and `Python`. The basic data types we'd like to support are:

| HDF5 | R | Python |
|:-----:|:-----:|:-----:|
|?| character | str |
|?|integer|int, np.int*, np.uint*|
|?|double|float, np.float*|
|?|vector|list, np.array|
|?|matrix|np.matrix|
|?|array|np.array, list of lists|
|?|data.frame|pd.DataFrame|
|?|NaN|np.nan|
|?|Na|None|

`np` for `numpy`, `pd` for `pandas`. Here is a test on Python's end:

```python
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
data = {'charater': 'pcarbo', 
        'integer1': 1, 'integer2': np.uint8(1), 
        'double1': 1.0, 'double2': np.float16(1.0), 
        'vector1': [1,2,'gaow'], 'vector2': [1,2,3], 'vector3': np.array([1,2,3]),
        'matrix': np.matrix([[1,2],[3,4]]),
        'array1': np.array([[1,2],[3,4]]), 'array2': [[1,2],[3,4]],
        'dataframe': pd.DataFrame({'A': [1,2], 'B': [3,4]}, index=['row1', 'row2'])
       }
data['recursive'] = data
```

Here is the outcome in HDF5:

[test.h5.zip](https://github.com/stephenslab/dsc2/files/1627144/test.h5.zip)

I used this API from UChicago:

https://github.com/uchicago-cs/deepdish/blob/master/deepdish/io/hdf5io.py

But it would not be difficult, I presume, to customize. 

**A particular difficult case is `NULL/NA/NaN` in R. In Python there are only `None` and `NaN`, no `NULL`**. #25  

@pcarbo am I missing any? would be interesting if you can check that HDF5 in R. Hopefully that gives us some useful insides in how we make cross language format consistent.

I'm not sure if this is the right board to post on, but here goes! I'm interested in what would be a good way to go about making an endpoint "trusted" in terms of it's organization. This means that, if I have endpoint A and call it a "sregistry endpoint" I can be guaranteed to file a particular kind of organization and type of file. I can give it some configuration to determine the structure, and if I (or anyone else) try to do an operation that goes against the expected structure I am not allowed. I can then associate this predictability with a label for others to find. Even better, I can also set constraints like this for metadata, so I can be sure that these endpoints provide something that looks like an API for associated metadata (and I think worst case some json file named equivalently to the image could be stored alongside each image for reading it, albeit it's not queryable). The use case looks like this:

1. I create a local registry (an organized storage folder) of Singularity images. This means that an organization is expected of collection folders with images named based on URIs, and that no files other than Singularity will be found in there.
2. I register my endpoint for some discovery tool so other uses can easily find it. I don't give anyone write permission, but I give global read permission so anyone can grab my images.
3. Then when I am interested in getting images from others, I use the discovery endpoint to search and filter down to some image I'm interested in. I do a "pull" sort of operation and get the image into my space locally, **or** just save the endpoint where I found it and other metadata for later usage.

issue_title: Consider removing link and unlink locks in lieu of undefined concurrent behavior
issue_body:
Locks are problematic in many ways.  There are multiple issues filed about them.  Yet the also ensure safety, in so far as it confines the scope of and reasoning about the code.

For 4.3, at least for a first canary release, I'm open to experimenting with removing locks for link and unlink operations.  The *strict* limitation will be that running link/unlink operations concurrently (i.e. in two open terminals) against the same prefix will lead to likely broken and certainly undefined behavior.

issue_title: Getting index for platforms other than the one you're on
issue_body:
There used to be a way to do this: https://github.com/conda/conda/blob/4.1.x/conda/api.py#L21-L23

but that seems to be gone in 4.2: https://github.com/conda/conda/blob/4.2.x/conda/api.py#L10 - the platform argument is still there, but it does not do anything.

Is there an equivalent way with the new config?  I looked for both of the two functions (```normalize_urls``` and ```get_channel_urls```) but neither of them seem to exist.  Where are you appending the platform to the URL, and can we make the platform changeable?

This is essential for the new build system, which examines dependencies and computes the necessary builds for all platforms from one central platform, so I'm marking it as blocking.

issue_title: `activate` clobbers existing prompt format on Windows
issue_body:
I have a custom environment variable set: `PROMPT = $P$_$$$S`, which -- in Windows land -- makes the prompt look like this:
```
Microsoft Windows [Version 6.1.7601]
Copyright (c) 2009 Microsoft Corporation.  All rights reserved.

C:\Users\phobson
$ activate ftd34
```

This is pretty handy when I'm working on a long network path on a small laptop screen.

But after I activate the environment, it looks like this:
```
[ftd34] C:\Users\phobson>deactivate
Deactivating environment "ftd34"...

C:\Users\phobson>
```

I'm curious it it'd be possible to read in the the `PROMPT` and simply place the `[envname]` before it rather than re-writing it entirely.

Appreciate all the hard work that goes into this. Real life saver.

issue_title: anaconda version with 4.4.0+ not forming soft links for shared libraries
issue_body:
I am using anaconda version 4.2.0 right now which has by default soft links enabled. When I did a clone from this env then size was around 255 mb. Everything is fine until now.

Then I started working on anaconda 4.4.0. I have marked always_softlink parameter to be true. After this, size of clone is around 1.4 GB which is huge compare to 255 mb. After further debugging, I found out there are many shared libraries and some other files whose symlinks are not getting created even after always_softlink is true. One such example is here :

Clone version of 4.2.9 (which is forming soft symlink)
```
 ls -al  /usr/lib/envss/a-4.2.9.0/lib/python2.7/site-packages/astropy/io/fits/compression.so -> 
lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root  116 Jun 12 08:26 compression.so -> /usr/lib/a-4.2.0-py-2.7.13/pkgs/astropy-1.2.1-np111py27_0/lib/python2.7/site-packages/astropy/io/fits/compression.so
```

Clone version of 4.4.0 (which is not forming soft link)
```
 ls -al /home/ec2-user/envs/soft_clone/lib/python2.7/site-packages/astropy/io/fits/compression.so 
-rwxrwxr-x 1 root root 3781144 Mar 31  2017 /home/ec2-user/envs/soft_clone/lib/python2.7/site-packages/astropy/io/fits/compression.so
```

How can I resolve this symlink issue of shared libraries in new versions of anaconda?

issue_title: Self-hard-linked packages are never cleaned up
issue_body:
**I'm submitting a...** - [X] bug report - [ ] feature request ## Current Behavior I have been using conda for quite a while now and accumulated a lot of packages in `~/.conda/pkgs` directory. Today I have updated all my environments to the latest versions of packages expecting the dependencies to "converge" and pretty much 1-2 copies of each package (py2 and py3) to remain. To my surprise some older Python packages didn't get deleted even though I was sure no env referenced them. It seems that conda checks if a package is being used by checking the number of hardlinks for each file within the package, but it doesn't check if all the hardlinks point inside the same package and thus if a package contains hardlinked files it's never considered for deletion. ### Steps to Reproduce ``` conda create -n py34 python=3.4.3 conda env remove --name py34 conda clean -a ``` After running that please observe that `~/.conda/pkgs/python3.4.3-2` remains undeleted even though it is unused by any other environment. I've put together a snippet that groups files that have more than one hardlink by their inode and injected it into conda in `find_pkgs` to see where's the problem: ``` multilink_inodes = {} for root, dir, files in walk(join(pkgs_dir, pkg)): for fn in files: fullpath = join(root, fn) if lstat(fullpath).st_nlink == 1: continue inode = lstat(fullpath).st_ino try: x = multilink_inodes[inode] except KeyError: x = multilink_inodes[inode] = { 'paths': [fullpath], 'nlink': lstat(fullpath).st_nlink } else: x['paths'].append(fullpath) for inode in list(multilink_inodes): inode_info = multilink_inodes[inode] if len(inode_info['paths']) < inode_info['nlink']: del multilink_inodes[inode] if multilink_inodes: print('Package %s: self-linked files: %s' % (pkg, multilink_inodes)) ``` And it printed the following: ``` Package python-3.4.3-2: self-linked files: {1995127: {u'paths': [u'/home/immerrr/.conda/pkgs/python-3.4.3-2/bin/python3.4', u'/home/immerrr/.conda/pkgs/python-3.4.3-2/bin/python3.4m'], u'nlink': 2}} ``` Admittedly, newer Python versions don't exhibit this behaviour, but other packages can try hardlinking files by accident or on purpose. ## Expected Behavior `~/.conda/pkgs/python3.4.3-2` should be removed because it's unused by any other environment. ## Environment Information
`conda info`

```
Using Anaconda API: https://api.anaconda.org
Solving environment: failed

CondaHTTPError: HTTP 000 CONNECTION FAILED for url <https://conda.anaconda.org/conda-forge/osx-64/repodata.json>

An HTTP error occurred when trying to retrieve this URL.
HTTP errors are often intermittent, and a simple retry will get you on your way.
ConnectionError(ReadTimeoutError("HTTPSConnectionPool(host='conda.anaconda.org', port=443): Read timed out.",),)
```

Another one from chatting to @tswicegood at pycon:

Have the channel api be a REST request thing where you ask a channel what it knows about a package, eg:

```
GET /channel/mypackage

{..., 'platforms': ['0.5.0x', '10.1.1']}
```

So:
- be able to look up a package, rather than having to download the 'total set of available package'
- have a compact notation (the two element list above) for the range of available versions available, so you know whether this channel can serve you the version you require

issue_title: conda clone of root copies conda-env

issue_title: failure activating enviroment when submitted to nodes
issue_body:
When submitting jobs on a HPC cluster, from time to time i run into such errors
```
FileNotFoundError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: '/somepath/env/conda3/bin/conda'
```
when trying to submit jobs like this in the pipeline:
```
source /somepath/env/lib/miniconda3/bin/activate /somepath/env/conda3 && do_stuff
```

issue_title: Text messages are decoded incorrectly under python2
issue_body:
On python2, `Text` events will contain a unicode object that is incorrectly encoded. As an example, given the following server (using `websocket-server`):

```python
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
from websocket_server import WebsocketServer

def new_client(client, server):
    server.send_message_to_all("Caño")

server = WebsocketServer(1234, host='0.0.0.0')
server.set_fn_new_client(new_client)
server.run_forever()
```

and the following client

```python
import lomond

ws = lomond.WebSocket('ws://localhost:1234')
for event in ws:

```
Under python2 I get:
```
Connecting(url='ws://localhost:1234')
Connected(url='ws://localhost:1234')
Ready(<response HTTP/1.1 101 Switching Protocols>, protocol=None, extensions=set([]))
Poll()
Text(u'Ca\xf1o')
Poll()
```
It works fine in python3

Just wondering if there was something in the BAM format that suggests if you should return the read length when there is no paired read and TLEN == 0?

Example:
`samtools`

```bash
samtools view SRR1585557_sort_resort.bam chr10:138870-138900

```
`pysam`

issue_title: Set default timeout_override for delete_snapshots action
issue_body:
When using the `delete_snapshots` action, the default timeout of 30s is not sufficient. Actions which have to wait for the on an HTTP request for the Elasticsearch cluster to complete the action generally have a long default timeout, `delete_snapshots` is an exception to that.

### Expected Behavior
The default timeout for the `delete_snapshots` action should be sufficient for most common situations.

### Actual Behavior
Without setting a `timeout_override` option, the `delete_snapshots` action frequently fails. Relevant log entries for this failure:
```
2018-01-18 15:57:15,962 INFO      Preparing Action ID: 400, "delete_snapshots"
2018-01-18 15:57:15,967 INFO      Trying Action ID: 400, "delete_snapshots": Delete snapshots of active states of now read-only indices
2018-01-18 15:57:46,005 ERROR     Failed to complete action: delete_snapshots.  <class 'curator.exceptions.FailedExecution'>: Unable to get snapshot information from repository: gcs_backup_active.  Error: ConnectionTimeout caused by - ReadTimeoutError(HTTPConnectionPool(host=u'elasticsearch', port=9200): Read timed out. (read timeout=30))
```

### Steps to Reproduce the Problem
I don't know how reliable these steps will be, but I'll describe my situation.
  1. Run Elasticsearch in the Google Kubernetes Engine
  1. Use the `repository-gcs` plugin to store shapshots to google cloud storage.
  1. Index a moderate amount of log data, we're collecting about 150 events/second totalling about 6GB per day, using daily indices.
  1. Take frequent snapshots of these indices.
  1. After a significant number of snapshots have accumulated, configure Curator to delete old snapshots. My configuration is:
```yaml
      400:
        action: delete_snapshots
        description: Delete snapshots of active states of now read-only indices
        options:
          disable_action: False
          repository: gcs_backup_active
          ignore_empty_list: True
        filters:
        - filtertype: age
          source: creation_date
          direction: older
          unit: days
          unit_count: 3
```
  1. These `delete_snapshot` actions will fail with the log entries shown above.
  1. Add `timeout_override` to the options of this job extending the timeout enough to allow the actions to pass.

Relevant log entries from a successful execution with increased timeout:
```
2018-01-18 16:02:43,311 INFO      Preparing Action ID: 400, "delete_snapshots"
2018-01-18 16:02:43,315 INFO      Trying Action ID: 400, "delete_snapshots": Delete snapshots of active states of now read-only indices
2018-01-18 16:04:16,166 INFO      Deleting selected snapshots
2018-01-18 16:05:28,218 INFO      Deleting snapshot ssa_201801120407...
2018-01-18 16:06:24,931 INFO      Deleting snapshot ssa_201801120411...
2018-01-18 16:07:55,201 INFO      Deleting snapshot ssa_201801120455...
2018-01-18 16:08:42,957 INFO      Deleting snapshot ssa_201801120459...
2018-01-18 16:09:34,815 INFO      Deleting snapshot ssa_201801120503...
```

### Specifications

- Version:
  - Curator version 5.4.1
  - Elasticsearch version 5.5.1
- Platform: Google Kubernetes Engine (linux, docker)
- Storage Plugin: `repository-gcs`

issue_title: Compatibility with host parsing when sniffing host in elasticsearch ver 1.x
issue_body:
Host sniffing use "/_nodes/_all/clear" to get hosts information,
where address returned in elasticsearch ver 1.x in format `inet\[/IP:PORT\]` like
```
"transport_address" : "inet[/192.168.0.2:9300]"
```
while in ver 2.x in format `[HOSTNAME/]IP:PORT` like
```
"transport_address" : "192.168.0.2:9300"
```

Such case cause incompatibility with parsing host info using client ver 2.x in elasticsearch 1.x, which obstructs upgrading from 1.x to 2.x in some respects.

issue_title: Broken pipe exception during bulk insert
issue_body:
I am using AWS Elasticsearch for my task. For entry level nodes, AWS ES has a HTTP payload limit of `10MB`. Therefore I am using following setting in the `bulk` API which is using `streaming_bulk` under the hood.

~~~
res = helpers.bulk(es, actions, max_chunk_bytes=9000000)
~~~

This works perfectly fine until I pass a huge file of the size `14MB`. I am getting `broken pipe` exception. To be exact, this is the error message I am getting:

~~~
2017-12-31 03:56:39,631 WARNING POST https://<aws_es_endpoint>:443/_bulk [status:N/A request:0.345s]
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "elasticsearch/connection/http_requests.py", line 76, in perform_request
  File "requests/sessions.py", line 576, in send
  File "requests/adapters.py", line 426, in send
ConnectionError: ('Connection aborted.', error(32, 'Broken pipe'))
~~~

I found several [workarounds](https://stackoverflow.com/a/16865106) in StackOverflow for broken pipe exception, but nothing specific to Elasticsearch.

How can I fix this issue. One possible option is split the huge files into chunks of  `<10MB`. But I assume, its already taken care by the `streaming_bulk` API when we specify `max_chunk_bytes` parameter.

issue_title: sql server create - Consistently too many 504 error responses

issue_title: [Create Commands] Flags will be confusing in SDK
issue_body:
Example:
Create a vm from SDK (not CLI) using the Smart Create command for vm.
There is a availability_set parameter and a availability_set_type parameter.  
Set the availability_set parameter to an availability set ID.
Run the command.

Expected: vm is added to the availability set
Actual: the vm is not added to the availability set because the availability_set_type parameter was set to null (which defaults to 'none').

Possible solution: we make the flags (which are a limitation of ARM templates) required so users must fill them out and then look for the related field to set a value to.  We would need to hide that required-ness on the CLI side.

issue_title: VM: smart create template hard codes blob uri to be US cloud specific

[This line](https://github.com/Azure/azure-cli/blob/master/src/command_modules/azure-cli-vm/azure/cli/command_modules/vm/mgmt_vm/azuredeploy.json#L176) uses `.blob.core.windows.net`, which will not work in any other non Us cloud envs, say China Cloud, whose endpoint should be`.core.chinacloudapi.cn`
```json
 "defaultValue": "[concat('https://', split(parameters('storageAccount'), '/')[sub(length(split(parameters('storageAccount'), '/')), 1)], '.blob.core.windows.net/', parameters('storageContainerName'), '/', parameters('osDiskName'), '.vhd')]",
```

issue_title: Feature Request:  Az Login should open the web browser automatically on WSL

Example:  `taylorb@taylorb-sl:/mnt/c/Users/taylorb$ cmd.exe /c start "http:\\\\www.microsoft.com"` results in opening www.microsoft.com in the users default browser (chrome, edge, firefox etc...)

issue_title: [Please read] Concerning the 0.37.0 release and library split
issue_body:
Dear community,

As you may have already [noticed](https://github.com/Azure/azure-storage-python/issues/333), we've been working on splitting the library into different packages. The split allows users to install only the service that they need, and also increase the maintainability of the library, which will allow faster releases in the future. These packages were just released under version 0.37.0:

azure-storage-blob
azure-storage-file
azure-storage-queue
azure-storage-common

You may notice that azure-storage-table is missing from the list, and that is because it’s now being released under a different namespace. Please refer to the new [Github repo](https://github.com/Azure/azure-cosmosdb-python) and [Pypi]( https://pypi.python.org/pypi/azure-cosmosdb-table) for more info. 

Anything that was previously directly under azure.storage are now in the new package azure.storage.common, which is a requirement for the new service packages(blob, file, queue). For example, azure.storage.CloudStorageAccount becomes azure.storage.common.CloudStorageAccount. The previous azure-storage meta-package is now deprecated after this change.

We understand that this is a significantly breaking change, but it is necessary in order to move forward. We welcome any feedback that you might have, and please refer to readme for more info on how to install the new packages. 😄

issue_title: Not all validation exceptions are raised prior to beginning upload/download
issue_body:
A customer discovered that, when they do not have permissions to download a file, unless error logging is enabled they do not get an exception. Additionally, the client preemptively creates the empty file prior to doing the validation that it can actually download the file.

To fix this we need to fix the following:
1. Ensure that all terminating exceptions are ultimately raised to the caller (not just logged)
2. Ensure that all preconditions are met and validated prior to making any state changes to the local or remote filesystems (don't create empty files before verifying that the actual files can be obtained). 

Note that for 2 this is best effort, since for a long running operation the user could lose permissions in the middle of the download/upload, at which point the client should fail with the right permissions error, but the state of the upload/download will be necessarily inconsistent.

issue_title: GunicornWebWorker: worker_abort not called on WORKER TIMEOUT event
issue_body:
## Long story short

In #1420, code was added to call the `worker_abort` callback when receiving the `SIGABRT` signal.

It works correctly, except when the worker is *blocked by a sync call*, which is typically the case on `WORKER TIMEOUT` event.

Thus, it is hard to catch such timeouts and send an error report.

## Expected behaviour

* In its loop running every second, Gunicorn's `Arbiter.murder_workers` detects a worker that was silent for too long (`timeout` setting)
* the arbiter calls `self.kill_worker(pid, signal.SIGABRT)` which should be received by `GunicornWebWorker.handle_abort`
* the `worker_abort` callback defined in the gunicorn config file should be called

## Actual behaviour

* the arbiter calls `self.kill_worker(pid, signal.SIGABRT)` on a blocked worker, nothing happens immediately
* the arbiter runs its loop 1 second later, and calls `self.kill_worker(pid, signal.SIGKILL)`
* the `GunicornWebWorker` worker is killed without calling any callbacks

## Idea to fix

Around the following code:
 https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp/blob/74810c2f9fdd459fadde0ae3d43c06dcae58064f/aiohttp/worker.py#L136-L137

Using `signal.signal(signal.SIGABRT, self.handle_abort)` instead of `self.loop.add_signal_handler(signal.SIGABRT, self.handle_abort, signal.SIGABRT, None)` may help, but I don't know how important it is to keep the signal handling attached to the event loop.

## Steps to reproduce

server.py:
```python
from aiohttp import web
import time

async def index(request):
    time.sleep(5)
    return web.Response(text='Hello Aiohttp!')

app = web.Application()
app.router.add_get('/', index)
```

gunicorn_config.py:
```python
worker_class = "aiohttp.GunicornWebWorker"
accesslog = "-"
errorlog = "-"

def worker_abort(worker):
    worker.log.info("CONFIG: worker received SIGABRT signal")
```

* run `gunicorn server:app --timeout 2  --config gunicorn_config.py`
* open the / url
* `worker_abort` is not called despite the `WORKER TIMEOUT` event


## Your environment

* ubuntu 14.04
* python 3.6
* gunicorn (19.7.1), aiohttp (3.0.2) (both up-to-date)

Thanks !

I've booked aiohttp.com, aiohttp.org and aiohttp.io
What name should be used: aiohttp.org or aiohttp.io?
aiohttp.com should be a redirection obviously.

I was testing out the command to run jupyter notebook to serve notebooks from a specific directory. As a test, I was trying to serve from C:\ (this is clearly not a sensible place to serve from normally, but it was a quick and easy test). But the command ignored the ```--notebook-dir``` option and served from the current directory.

```
    >jupyter notebook --notebook-dir=C:\
    [I 11:05:43.435 NotebookApp] Serving notebooks from local directory: C:\Work\Scratch
    [I 11:05:43.435 NotebookApp] 0 active kernels
    [I 11:05:43.435 NotebookApp] The Jupyter Notebook is running at: http://localhost:8888/
    [I 11:05:43.435 NotebookApp] Use Control-C to stop this server and shut down all kernels (twice to skip confirmation).
    [I 11:05:58.443 NotebookApp] Interrupted...
    [I 11:05:58.443 NotebookApp] Shutting down kernels
```

This is Jupyter 4.0.6, on Windows 7, using Python 3.5.1 64-bit (python.org version).

It appears to only be ```C:\``` that has this behaviour - other directories work fine with or without a trailing backslash, and with back or forward slashes. Also, ```C:/``` works as expected. Oddly, even using ```D:\``` works, so it's somehow specific to the C drive.

It's clearly a very minor corner case, but I wasted quite some time working out what was going on.
